questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
n + 14 = 31
questions.label: n =

questions.stem:
Find the value of m.
5m = 60
questions.label: m =

questions.stem: Work out 4p + 3 when p = 5.
questions.label: Answer =

questions.stem:
Continue the sequence. Write the next two terms.
3, 8, 13, 18, ___, ___

A plumber uses the formula C = 3h + 5 to work out his charge (C = total cost in £, h = hours worked).
What is the charge for 4 hours?
questions.label: C = £

questions.stem:
Amir has n sweets. He gives 4 away.
Which expression shows how many he has left?
questions.solution: He gives 4 away, so we subtract: n − 4.

questions.stem:
Find the value of t.
3t − 4 = 20
questions.label: t =

questions.stem: Work out c² + d when c = 3 and d = 8.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: c² = 3 × 3 = 9.   Then 9 + 8 = 17.   Remember: square first, then add!

questions.stem:
What is the rule for this sequence?
64, 32, 16, 8, ...
questions.solution: Rule: divide by 2 (halve) each time.   Next terms: 4, 2, 1.

questions.stem:
a and b are positive whole numbers and a > b.
a + b = 10
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution:
Any pair where a + b = 10 and a > b works:
a = 6, b = 4  |  a = 7, b = 3  |  a = 8, b = 2  |  a = 9, b = 1

questions.stem:
Find the value of k.
6k + 5 = 41
questions.label: k =

questions.stem: Work out 3a² when a = 4.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: a² = 4 × 4 = 16.   Then 3 × 16 = 48.   Square first, then multiply!

questions.stem:
Continue the sequence. Write the next two terms.
5, 9, 13, 17, ___, ___

The formula is F = 2m + 4.
If F = 20, find m.
questions.label: m =

questions.stem:
A bag has n apples. 6 more are added.
Which expression shows the total?
questions.solution: 6 more are added, so we add: n + 6.

questions.stem:
The formula for the perimeter of a square is P = 4l.
What is the perimeter when l = 7?
questions.label: P =

questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
48 ÷ n = 6
questions.label: n =

questions.stem: Work out 5x − y when x = 6 and y = 9.
questions.label: Answer =

questions.stem:
Continue the sequence. Write the next two terms.
96, 48, 24, 12, ___, ___

x and y are positive whole numbers and x < 20.
x − y = 6
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution:
Any pair where x − y = 6 and x < 20 works:
x = 7, y = 1  |  x = 8, y = 2  |  x = 9, y = 3  |  x = 10, y = 4  (and more)

questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
2n + 7 = 19
questions.label: n =

questions.stem: Work out 6m − 4 when m = 3.
questions.label: Answer =

questions.stem:
Continue the sequence. Write the next two terms.
2, 6, 18, 54, ___, ___
Work out (a + b) × 3 when a = 4 and b = 6.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: Brackets first: (4 + 6) = 10.   Then 10 × 3 = 30.   Always work out brackets first (BIDMAS)!

questions.stem:
y pencils are shared equally between 3 children.
Which expression shows how many pencils each child gets?
questions.solution: Shared equally between 3 means divide: y ÷ 3.

questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
n² = 49
questions.label: n =
questions.solution: n² means n × n.   7 × 7 = 49, so n = 7.   Check: 7² = 49 ✓

questions.stem:
A formula for the area of a rectangle is A = l × w.
What is the area when l = 8 and w = 5?
questions.label: A =

questions.stem:
The sequence 4, 7, 10, 13, … follows a rule.
Write an expression for the nth term.
questions.label: nth term =
questions.solution: The rule adds 3 each time, so start with 3n.   When n = 1: 3(1) = 3, but we need 4, so add 1.   nth term = 3n + 1.   Check: n=2 → 3(2)+1 = 7 ✓

questions.stem:
What is the rule for this sequence?
1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ...
questions.solution: These are square numbers: 1², 2², 3², 4², 5², …   Next terms: 36, 49, 64.

questions.stem:
a and b are positive whole numbers.
2a + b = 11
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution:
Any pair where 2a + b = 11 works:
a = 1, b = 9  |  a = 2, b = 7  |  a = 3, b = 5  |  a = 4, b = 3  |  a = 5, b = 1

questions.stem:
Find the value of t.
4t + 9 = 37
questions.label: t =

questions.stem: Work out p² − q when p = 5 and q = 7.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: p² = 5 × 5 = 25.   Then 25 − 7 = 18.   Square first, then subtract!

questions.stem:
Continue the sequence. Write the next two terms.
3, 7, 11, 15, ___, ___

A taxi firm uses the formula C = 45m + 20 to calculate the cost in pence (C = cost, m = miles).
What is the cost for 2 miles?
questions.label: C =

questions.stem:
A box holds n biscuits. There are 5 boxes.
Which expression shows the total number of biscuits?
questions.solution: 5 groups of n biscuits means multiply: 5n (which means 5 × n).

questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
n ÷ 4 + 3 = 8
questions.label: n =

questions.stem: Work out 2(x + y) when x = 5 and y = 3.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: Brackets first: (5 + 3) = 8.   Then 2 × 8 = 16.   Always work out brackets first (BIDMAS)!

questions.stem:
The sequence 5, 8, 11, 14, … follows a rule.
Write an expression for the nth term.
questions.label: nth term =
questions.solution: The rule adds 3 each time, so start with 3n.   When n = 1: 3(1) = 3, but we need 5, so add 2.   nth term = 3n + 2.   Check: n=2 → 3(2)+2 = 8 ✓

questions.stem:
What is the rule for this sequence?
1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ...
questions.solution: Rule: multiply by 2 (double) each time.   Next terms: 32, 64, 128.

questions.stem:
m and n are positive whole numbers.
m × n = 12
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution:
Any factor pair of 12 works:
m = 1, n = 12  |  m = 2, n = 6  |  m = 3, n = 4  |  m = 4, n = 3  |  m = 6, n = 2  |  m = 12, n = 1

questions.stem:
Find the value of n.
3n − 5 = 22
questions.label: n =

questions.stem:
Find the missing first term.
___, 7, 13, 19, 25
questions.label: Missing term =
questions.solution: Rule: add 6 each time.   Working backwards: 7 − 6 = 1.   Check: 1, 7, 13, 19, 25 ✓

questions.stem:
The nth term of a sequence is 4n + 3.
What is the 8th term?
questions.label: 8th term =

questions.stem:
Find the nth term of this sequence.
11, 19, 27, 35, …
questions.label: nth term =
questions.solution: Increases by 8 each time → start with 8n.   When n = 1: 8(1) = 8, but we need 11, so add 3.   nth term = 8n + 3.   Check: n = 2 → 8(2) + 3 = 19 ✓

questions.stem:
A sequence has the rule 6n + 1.
What is the 10th term?
questions.label: 10th term =

questions.stem:
Find the missing term.
3, ___, 15, 21, 27
questions.label: Missing term =

questions.stem: Work out 3(p − q) when p = 8 and q = 3.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: Brackets first: (8 − 3) = 5.   Then 3 × 5 = 15.   Always work out brackets first (BIDMAS)!

questions.stem:
Which expression is the nth term of this sequence?
2, 7, 12, 17, …
questions.solution: Increases by 5 → start with 5n.   When n = 1: 5(1) = 5, but we need 2, so subtract 3.   nth term = 5n − 3.   Check: n = 2 → 10 − 3 = 7 ✓

questions.stem:
The nth term of a sequence is 8n + 3.
A term in the sequence is 43. What is its position (n)?
questions.label: n =

questions.stem:
a and b are positive whole numbers.
3a + 2b = 16
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution: Valid pairs:   a = 2, b = 5  |  a = 4, b = 2

questions.stem:
Find the value of w.
5w − 7 = 43
questions.label: w =

questions.stem:
Find the missing first term.
___, 15, 24, 33, 42
questions.label: Missing term =
questions.solution: Rule: add 9 each time.   Working backwards: 15 − 9 = 6.   Check: 6, 15, 24, 33, 42 ✓

questions.stem:
The nth term of a sequence is 3n − 4.
What is the 12th term?
questions.label: 12th term =

questions.stem:
Find the missing term.
4, ___, 22, 31, 40
questions.label: Missing term =

questions.stem:
Find the nth term of this sequence.
6, 11, 16, 21, …
questions.label: nth term =
questions.solution: Increases by 5 → start with 5n.   When n = 1: 5(1) = 5, but we need 6, so add 1.   nth term = 5n + 1.   Check: n = 2 → 5(2) + 1 = 11 ✓

questions.stem:
The nth term of a sequence is 7n − 2.
What is the 6th term?
questions.label: 6th term =

questions.stem:
Which expression is the nth term of this sequence?
3, 9, 15, 21, …
questions.solution: Increases by 6 → start with 6n.   When n = 1: 6(1) = 6, but we need 3, so subtract 3.   nth term = 6n − 3.   Check: n = 2 → 12 − 3 = 9 ✓

questions.stem:
The nth term of a sequence is 9n + 2.
A term in the sequence is 56. What is its position (n)?
questions.label: n =

questions.stem: Work out 4x² when x = 3.
questions.label: Answer =
questions.solution: x² = 3 × 3 = 9.   Then 4 × 9 = 36.   Square first, then multiply!

questions.stem:
p and q are positive whole numbers and p < q.
p + q = 15
Write one pair of values that works.
questions.solution:
Any pair where p + q = 15 and p < q works:
p = 1, q = 14  |  p = 2, q = 13  |  p = 3, q = 12  |  p = 4, q = 11  |  p = 5, q = 10  |  p = 6, q = 9  |  p = 7, q = 8
